commands.review_summary:
The PR validates and normalizes `screen.snapshot` formats, derives the response data URI MIME type from the validated format, and adds shared tests plus test README updates.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current `main` interpolates `ScreenCaptureResult.Format` directly into the `data:image/...` response and the backend echoes `args.Format`. I did not execute the Windows runtime path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer action is needed for real behavior proof, draft readiness, final checks, and compatibility acceptance; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to queue.

target.title: Harden screen snapshot format handling
target.body:
## Summary

Harden `screen.snapshot` format handling before screen capture runs.

- Validate requested snapshot formats against `png`, `jpeg`, and `jpg` (normalized to `jpeg`).
- Reject unsupported formats before invoking the capture backend.
- Build the response `data:image/...` MIME type from the validated format instead of the backend echo.
- Add coverage for unsupported formats, alias normalization, and backend-echo hardening.

## Why

`screen.snapshot` includes a data URI in its response. Previously the requested/echoed format could flow into that MIME type, which could cause MIME confusion or advertise a format that did not match the encoded bytes. This keeps the advertised MIME type aligned with the supported encoder output.

## Behavioral proof

Local harness invoked `ScreenCapability.ExecuteAsync` directly with a stub capture backend:

```text
CASE 1: unsupported format rejected before capture
ok=False
error=Unsupported screen snapshot format. Supported formats: png, jpeg.
handlerCalls=0

CASE 2: jpg alias normalizes to jpeg
ok=True
handlerReceivedFormat=jpeg
payloadFormat=jpeg
image=data:image/jpeg;base64,abc123

CASE 3: backend Format echo cannot affect data URI MIME
ok=True
backendEchoFormat=svg+xml";base64,evil
payloadFormat=png
image=data:image/png;base64,abc123
```

This proves the handler is not called for unsupported formats, `jpg` is normalized before capture, and a malicious backend `Format` echo cannot change the response MIME type.

## Validation

- `./build.ps1` ✅ all 5 projects
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj` ✅ 2426 passed / 0 failed
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj` ✅ 1163 passed / 0 failed
